title: Mellow Ukrainian Cheonggukjang Dumplings
description: A mild, malty dumpling crafted from fermented soybean paste, gentle masa and heart‑warming broth, perfect for a relaxed brunch.
instructions: In a bowl, combine flour, salt, and water and knead until a smooth dough forms.
Rest the dough for 10 minutes covered with a damp cloth.
In a separate bowl, mix cheonggukjang, soy sauce, ginger, cumin, grated carrot, chopped onion, dill, and sesame oil until well combined.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a 4-inch circle with a rolling pin.
Place a teaspoon of the cheonggukjang filling in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and pinch the edges to seal, forming a half-moon shape.
Place the dumplings on a lightly oiled tray and steam over boiling water for 12 minutes.
Remove the dumplings from the steamer and serve warm with a small bowl of light broth or yogurt.
